name: aligned_zero_size
reason: If aligned(addr, 0) is provable, the spec fails to guard against zero-size alignment (division by zero)

name: upper_vaddr_equals_base
code:
proof fn phi_2_upper_vaddr_equals_base(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.upper_vaddr(layer, base) == base,
{
}
reason: If upper_vaddr equals base under inv, the mapped address range is zero-sized, meaning no virtual addresses are covered

name: entry_base_collision
code:
proof fn phi_3_entry_base_collision(arch: Arch, layer: nat, base: nat, i: nat, j: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        layer < arch.layers.len(),
        i < arch.num_entries(layer),
        j < arch.num_entries(layer),
        i != j,
    ensures
        arch.entry_base(layer, base, i) == arch.entry_base(layer, base, j),
{
}
reason: If distinct indices yield the same entry base, page table entries would collide and the translation structure is unsound

name: entry_size_hierarchy_inversion
code:
proof fn phi_4_entry_size_hierarchy_inversion(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i + 1 < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.entry_size((i + 1) as nat) >= arch.entry_size(i),
{
}
reason: If a child layer's entry size is >= its parent's, the hierarchical page table decomposition is broken

name: inv_allows_zero_num_entries
code:
proof fn phi_5_inv_allows_zero_num_entries(arch: Arch, i: nat)
    requires
        arch.inv(),
        i < arch.layers.len(),
    ensures
        arch.num_entries(i) == 0,
{
}
reason: If inv permits a layer with zero entries, the architecture admits degenerate empty layers that cannot map any addresses
